heading: Failure of applicable plans reducing benefit accruals to satisfy notice requirements
label: 4980F Failure of applicable plans reducing benefit accruals to satisfy notice requirements

children.heading: Imposition of tax
children.label: a Imposition of tax
children.text: There is hereby imposed a tax on the failure of any applicable pension plan to meet the requirements of subsection (e) with respect to any applicable individual.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The amount of the tax imposed by subsection (a) on any failure with respect to any applicable individual shall be $100 for each day in the noncompliance period with respect to such failure.

children.heading: Noncompliance period
children.label: 2 Noncompliance period
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “noncompliance period” means, with respect to any failure, the period beginning on the date the failure first occurs and ending on the date the notice to which the failure relates is provided or the failure is otherwise corrected.

children.heading: Limitations on amount of tax
children.label: c Limitations on amount of tax

children.heading: Tax not to apply where failure not discovered and reasonable diligence exercised
children.label: 1 Tax not to apply where failure not discovered and reasonable diligence exercised
children.text: No tax shall be imposed by subsection (a) on any failure during any period for which it is established to the satisfaction of the Secretary that any person subject to liability for the tax under subsection (d) did not know that the failure existed and exercised reasonable diligence to meet the requirements of subsection (e).

children.heading: Tax not to apply to failures corrected within 30 days
children.label: 2 Tax not to apply to failures corrected within 30 days
A
children.text: any person subject to liability for the tax under subsection (d) exercised reasonable diligence to meet the requirements of subsection (e), and

children.label: B
children.text: such person provides the notice described in subsection (e) during the 30-day period beginning on the first date such person knew, or exercising reasonable diligence would have known, that such failure existed.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If the person subject to liability for tax under subsection (d) exercised reasonable diligence to meet the requirements of subsection (e), the tax imposed by subsection (a) for failures during the taxable year of the employer (or, in the case of a multiemployer plan, the taxable year of the trust forming part of the plan) shall not exceed $500,000. For purposes of the preceding sentence, all multiemployer plans of which the same trust forms a part shall be treated as 1 plan.

children.heading: Taxable years in the case of certain controlled groups
children.label: B Taxable years in the case of certain controlled groups
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, if all persons who are treated as a single employer for purposes of this section do not have the same taxable year, the taxable years taken into account shall be determined under principles similar to the principles of section 1561.

children.heading: Waiver by Secretary
children.label: 4 Waiver by Secretary
children.text: In the case of a failure which is due to reasonable cause and not to willful neglect, the Secretary may waive part or all of the tax imposed by subsection (a) to the extent that the payment of such tax would be excessive or otherwise inequitable relative to the failure involved.
In the case of a plan other than a multiemployer plan, the employer.
In the case of a multiemployer plan, the plan.

children.heading: Notice requirements for plans significantly reducing benefit accruals
children.label: e Notice requirements for plans significantly reducing benefit accruals

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: If an applicable pension plan is amended to provide for a significant reduction in the rate of future benefit accrual, the plan administrator shall provide the notice described in paragraph (2) to each applicable individual (and to each employee organization representing applicable individuals) and to each employer who has an obligation to contribute to the plan.

children.label: A
children.text: which has fewer than 100 participants who have accrued a benefit under the plan, or

children.label: B
children.text: which offers participants the option to choose between the new benefit formula and the old benefit formula.

children.heading: Timing of notice
children.label: 3 Timing of notice
children.text: Except as provided in regulations, the notice required by paragraph (1) shall be provided within a reasonable time before the effective date of the plan amendment.

children.heading: Designees
children.label: 4 Designees
children.text: Any notice under paragraph (1) may be provided to a person designated, in writing, by the person to which it would otherwise be provided.

children.heading: Notice before adoption of amendment
children.label: 5 Notice before adoption of amendment
children.text: A plan shall not be treated as failing to meet the requirements of paragraph (1) merely because notice is provided before the adoption of the plan amendment if no material modification of the amendment occurs before the amendment is adopted.

children.label: A
children.text: each participant in the plan, and

children.label: B
children.text: any beneficiary who is an alternate payee (within the meaning of section 414(p)(8)) under an applicable qualified domestic relations order (within the meaning of section 414(p)(1)(A)),

children.label: A
children.text: any defined benefit plan described in section 401(a) which includes a trust exempt from tax under section 501(a), or

children.label: B
children.text: an individual account plan which is subject to the funding standards of section 412.

children.heading: Early retirement
children.label: 3 Early retirement
children.text: A plan amendment which eliminates or reduces any early retirement benefit or retirement-type subsidy (within the meaning of section 411(d)(6)(B)(i)) shall be treated as having the effect of reducing the rate of future benefit accrual.

children.heading: New technologies
children.label: g New technologies
children.text: The Secretary may by regulations allow any notice under subsection (e) to be provided by using new technologies.
